entry.title: Re: Filter Block using Calculated Measure (RunningSum, Ra...
entry.content:
create a filter-variable that is resulting in a "0" or "1", including all your conditions/calculations.
insert this variable into your report to see what is going on in each line. 
check if the lines are shown as "1" when matching your requirement, and "0" if you want to hide this line.
now you can set a filter to the block "variable = 1"  that will hide all non-matching-lines.

helpful tip:
to check/find the correct formula in your filter-variable it will be helpful to insert every single condition in a variable
and insert all these in your report to see if every condition is working separately as expected.

entry.title: Group Reporting - Release 2602 is generally available
entry.content:
With each quarterly release, Group Reporting continues to expand its capabilities to help you automate, streamline, and modernize your consolidation processes. After introducing the new consolidation rule engine in release 2508, we are further enhancing this framework in release 2602, bringing additional flexibility, broader automation coverage, and improved transparency for your group-level postings - including key scenarios such as the consolidation of investments.

Release 2602 also delivers a wide set of innovations across the financial consolidation process: extended rule engine functions, IFRS 18‑ready content, deeper integration with SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Datasphere, improvements in Group Reporting Data Collection, strengthened controls for manual postings, and new analytics integration features.

In the sections below, you’ll find an overview of the main enhancements delivered with 2602 and how they can support you in optimizing your financial close and reporting processes.

Download Group Reporting 2602 feature deck

-
New Consolidation Rule Engine

Since August 2025 (release 2508) you benefit from a new rule engine to automate your consolidation postings. This engine is inspired by SAP Financial Consolidation and aims to offer the same level of power and flexibility valued by its users. With release 2602, we continue to enhance the consolidation engine, giving you more flexibility, more automation possibilities, and clearer insight in your group-level postings - including the consolidation of investments. 

Below is an overview of the new capabilities. 

Flexible rules to automate consolidation postings

The enhanced rule engine now supports a broader range of consolidation scenarios, allowing you to define precise and reusable rules for group-level postings.

​These rules can be used to automate:

  • Purchase, equity method
  • First consolidation
  • Subsequent consolidation
  • Changes in equity and changes in investments
  • Method changes
  • Divestiture

You can also apply them to other group postings, such as reclassifications or KPI calculations.

More information: 

Variables, functions and operators to automate complex eliminations

You can now design more sophisticated logic using variables, functions, and operators. This enables complex calculations based on multiple data sources.

Examples include:

  • Non-controlling interests
  • Direct ownership % (owned shares / issued shares)
  • Earning per share
  • Profit in inventory elimination using margin rates

Sample formulas:

  • SOURCE (Source amounts) / ISSSHA (Nb of issued shares) => Earning per shares 
  • 100 * SOURCE_QTY (Source quantity) / ISSSHA_P (Nb of issued shares - partner unit) => Direct ownership %
These enhancements allow you to model more advanced elimination scenarios directly in the rule engine.

Enhanced logs for investee unit analysis

The new log capabilities make auditing faster and clearer.

You now benefit from:

  • A dedicated Investee Unit tab
  • Grouped log items by investee unit
  • Display of variable values in the log
  • Quick navigation to group structure assignment details

This provides full transparency into how rules are applied across investor-investee relationships.

Define your own consolidation methods

You can now create your own consolidation methods using Self Service Configuration. 

Once created:

  1. Assign the method to consolidation units in "Manage Group Structure"
  2. Use the method in "Group Structure Filters"
  3. Build rules specific to your new method in "Manage Rules for Automatic Postings"
This enables more flexible modeling of consolidation scenarios, including proportional consolidation or other custom approaches.

Pre-delivered content

​Release 2602 delivers new rule groups and rules to help you accelerate your setup, including:

  • Net income calculation (released and reported data)
  • Intercompany eliminations
    - Sales
    - Operating income & expenses
    - Financial income & expenses
    - Profit in inventory
    - Dividends
    - Assets & liabilities
These templates provide a strong starting point for common consolidation requirements.

OData API to read consolidation selections

​A new OData v4 API allows you to read consolidation selections - reusable sets of master data filtered by items, units, attributes, or hierarchy nodes.

This enables integration and reporting scenarios that depend on consolidation selections across:

  • Validation rules
  • Reclassification methods
  • Currency translation methods
  • Breakdown categories

More information

More information on the consolidation rule engine capabilities: 

  • Using Automatic Posting Rules for Consolidation of Investments
  • Rules for Automatic Postings

-
IFRS 18 Presentation and Disclosure in Financial Statements

IFRS 18 was published on April 9, 2024 and will replace IAS1 Presentation of Financial Statements. IFRS 18 aims to enhance the comparability of financial statements across companies. It's effective for annual reporting periods beginning on or after January 1, 2027. Year 2026 must be presented as comparative figures.

Key changes:

  • Structure of the income statement 
  • Management-defined performance measures
  • Aggregation and disaggregation
  • Cash flow statement

Unchanged requirements in IAS 1 have been transferred to IFRS 18 and other standards.

Pre-delivered content for IFRS 18

To help you comply with this new standard, we deliver pre-defined content for IFRS 18. New cloud customers will receive these compliant configurations by default. ​Existing cloud customers won't receive these updates automatically by default, to safeguard your unique system customizations from being overwritten.

Above is an example of the pre-delivered content, with the update structure of the consolidated Profit or Loss Statement, where IFRS 18 requires:

  • Blue: present two new defined subtotals - operating profit and profit before financing and income taxes
  • Green: classify income and expenses into operating, investing and financing categories in the statement of profit or loss - plus income taxes and discontinued operations

More information: IFRS 18 and Group Reporting: What It Means and How to Get Ahead and SAP Note 3694359 - How to Adopt IFRS 18 for Financial Reporting in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

-
Integration with SAP Business Data Cloud

Group Reporting data products

Data products in SAP Business Data Cloud provide a standardized, high‑quality way to share and consume data across applications and domains, enabling efficient, replication‑free integration. They're optimized for analytics and AI through curated, read‑optimized storage, rich business metadata, and exposure via APIs, events, or delta sharing. Designed with a data‑mesh mindset, they support decentralized ownership and a full lifecycle approach to ensure relevance and reliability.

As of release 2602, the following content is available for Group Reporting in the Business Data Cloud:

  • Data products for Group Reporting master data and raw transactional data
  • Extraction of Group Reporting master data (values, descriptions, attributes, hierarchies)
  • Extraction of Group Reporting raw transactional data

For a complete view of the delivered Group Reporting data products, please check the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Integration with SAP Datasphere

By integrating SAP Datasphere as the centralized business data fabric with Group Reporting, you can effectively eliminate upstream data bottlenecks. Datasphere’s powerful modeling capabilities allow for intelligent, unified data mapping, ensuring the perfect alignment of all source data, including SAP and non-SAP inputs, to the Group Reporting Data Model. The result is an accelerated data readiness, a superior auditability, and an accelerated close cycle.

The integration of Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC) with SAP Datasphere includes:

  • Support Datasphere as a source type
  • Use Datasphere destinations created in SAP BTP Cockpit as source instances in data mapping definitions
  • Use Datasphere Spaces (workspaces that hold data models and views) and Assets (specific datasets or objects inside a space)
  • Provide templates to easily create Datasphere data mapping file
  • Run data mapping jobs to connect to Datasphere, extract, transform and load data into the target consolidation table (ACDOCU)

For more information, check out the article Accelerating Financial Close: Integrating SAP Datasphere with SAP Group Reporting and Create a Data Mapping Definition with SAP Datasphere as Source

-
Balance Validation in Manual Posting

You can run balance validation checks on a draft document that was created by a manual posting in the Post Group Journal Entries or in the Import Group Journal Entries app. 

To do so, you activate the balance validation for the required manual posting tasks in the configuration and assign the balance validation rules to these tasks. If the validation isn't successful, the system blocks the posting. A corresponding error message is displayed, and you can navigate to the balance validation log to view more details.

The system starts the balance validation when you choose Create or Check in the Post Group Journal Entries or in the Import Group Journal Entries app:

  • The task is derived from the document type
  • If a group journal has extension versions, the system only validates the main version
  • If a group journal has multiple consolidation groups (posting level 30), the system only validates the primary consolidation group
  • Depending on the result of the validation, a success or error message is displayed, and you can navigate to the balance validation log to view further detail
  • If an error is detected, the posting of the group journal is not possible

More information: Balance Validation for Manual Posting

-
Data Collection

You can benefit from a series of improvements and new features in Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC): importing data directly from the package, and benefit from an enhanced traceability log in the Data Mapping app. In addition you can now import SAP Analytics Cloud data more easily. 

Pull Data from SAP Analytics Cloud

With release 2602, you can now import SAP Analytics Cloud data more easily (for example plan data):

  • New tasks for pull data from SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • Proceed these pull tasks from Group Reporting's Data Monitor
  • New app for SAP Analytics Cloud model and field association
  • Ability to schedule the task with a new job template in the app for scheduling jobs for consolidation tasks

More information: Importing Planning Data from SAP Analytics Cloud in Group Reporting

Import data directly from your manual entry package

You can now perform data import directly from within the GRDC package: data mapping jobs are integrated in the package. 

More information: Run a Job from Within a Package

Improvements to the traceability log

In Data Mapping, from the Run page you can now choose to only download the rejected items in the traceability log.

More information: Traceability Logs

Pull Data from SAP Analytics Cloud

With release 2602, you can now import SAP Analytics Cloud data more easily (for example plan data):

  • New tasks for pull data from SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • Proceed these pull tasks from Group Reporting's Data Monitor
  • New app for SAP Analytics Cloud model and field association
  • Ability to schedule the task with a new job template in the app for scheduling jobs for consolidation tasks

More information: Importing Planning Data from SAP Analytics Cloud in Group Reporting

-
Analytics - Navigate in Group View on Accounting

Navigate in Group View on Accounting

You can now navigate to group financial statements from Group Reporting based columns.

More information: Group View on Accounting

-
Other innovations

Collect

  • Balance validation: overwrite rule control level by a new option in the self-service configuration user interface
  • Authorization restriction by activity in the data monitor
  • Schedule custom tasks in the data monitor
  • GRDC data mapping - group journal entries import API
  • GRDC data mapping - source and target attributes in mapping files
  • GRDC data mapping - keywords in source filters and scheduled job

Analytics

  • Time-dependent nodes and leaves in hierarchies

-
More information

  • Group Reporting 2602 feature deck
  • Group Reporting 2602 help portal
  • Recap of Group Reporting new features
  • Group reporting community
  • Group reporting customer influence platform

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / Navigation from Notifications...
entry.content:
Dear @LukasKuhn,

In My Inbox Expert Mode isn’t a different workflow inbox service it’s the same intent (navigation target) started with an extra startup parameter for example expertMode=true. SAP even documents configuring a My Inbox tile/target mapping with Semantic Object = WorkflowTask, Action = displayInbox and adding expertMode=true to start in the table expert view

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/0182011d28a34c99b57b65464ab11b14/2.0%202016-08/en-US/41fd595461fce630e10000000a44538d.html

When you click a launchpad notification (the bell) the launchpad navigates using the navigation target (semantic object/action + parameters) contained in the notification payload.
For workflow task notifications that navigation typically does not include expertMode=true so the app starts in the default normal view

Can you change what opens from the notification bell in Public Cloud?

NOT in a supported/standard way. To force Expert View from the bell you would need SAP notification navigation to either:

  • include expertMode=true in the notification target parameters or
  • have the launchpad resolve the intent #WorkflowTask-displayInbox to a target mapping that adds a default expertMode=true

Summary:

In Public Cloud you generally can’t change SAP-delivered notification navigation or safely override the standard intent resolution for workflow task notifications system-wide without running into conflicts/upgrade issues

BUT you can try one of the methods below, it may bring the desired effect:

  • SAP Customer Influence (submit/vote for improvement requests)

More details below:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/influence-adopt

  • SAP Support

More details below:

https://support.sap.com/en/index.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: MIGO Not Opening in Browser After MB03 Replacement
entry.content:
Hello,

I 'm using this code:


CALL FUNCTION 'ABAP4_CALL_TRANSACTION'
DESTINATION des_id
STARTING NEW TASK v_task
EXPORTING
tcode = v_ttcode
skip_screen = abap_true
TABLES
spagpa_tab = lt_spagpa
EXCEPTIONS
call_transaction_denied = 1
tcode_invalid = 2
system_failure = 3 
communication_failure = 4 
OTHERS = 5.

Well, v_ttcode = 'MB03' and I got the popup that Transaction MB03 is obsolete(see note 210569) in SAP GUI.
I replaced the ABAP4_CALL_TRANSACTION with:
* CALL FUNCTION 'MIGO_DIALOG'
* DESTINATION v_des_id
* STARTING NEW TASK v_task
* EXPORTING
* i_mblnr = v_mblnr
* i_mjahr = v_mjahr

The problem is that it is working on SAP GUI like this but when calling from browser/portal , it does not work. Calling means clicking on a ALV link(mblnr). Since it is a dialog function module it will not work.

I tried also using v_ttcode = 'MIGO' while calling it with  ABAP4_CALL_TRANSACTION but still not opening anything  from portal. At alv click nothing happened. I tried debugging, it just does nothing. 

Could anyone please suggest a solution? Is there a way to resolve this? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Clean Core in 2026: Can We Truly Achieve It Without a BTP‑First Mindset?
entry.content:
@Dominik_Tylczynski 

I appreciate the pragmatism—BTP is definitely a tool, not a miracle cure. However, my concern with staying 'on-stack' for custom logic is the technical debt we're piling up for the next upgrade cycle.

You mentioned the TM (Transportation Management) space—that's a great example. If you have a complex carrier selection logic that isn't in standard, do you prefer a classic BAdI, or have you explored Developer Extensibility (On-stack Cloud ABAP) to keep it 'Clean Core' compliant without leaving the S/4 environment? I’d be curious where you draw the line between a necessary enhancement and 'toxic' Z-code.    Appreciate your valuable thought here.

entry.title: Re: Oil and Gas Industry – SAP Profitability and Performance Management
entry.content:
Dear SAP,   In downstream scenarios, the economic value of the product is often generated outside S/4HANA by specialized applications that reconstruct the physical and economic chain across multiple facilities, including external installations, non‑linear movements, temporary negative inventories, and situations where physical traceability does not align with economic traceability.
In these cases, a single product may pass through several external facilities, be blended with other products, be transformed at different stages, or circulate through complex networks before reaching the ERP. The following questions arise for SAP:
1. What is the recommended architectural approach to integrate externally calculated downstream values into Material Ledger, while avoiding the need to reproduce within S/4HANA complex physical‑economic models, external installations, or manual adjustments that are not part of the SAP landscape?
2. What integration pattern does SAP propose to ensure correct IFRS valuation while maintaining physical traceability in S/4HANA and economic traceability in an external application, particularly in scenarios involving blends, transformations, or circular networks that make it difficult to obtain product‑level margins separately?
3. Does SAP provide any reference model, blueprint, or standard process in SAP Signavio Process Navigator or in the Solution Scenarios that represents a downstream scenario (including external installations, blends, transformations, or circular networks) within S/4HANA? If so, which components or building blocks does it include?
- Complementary Technical Requirement
Specifically, how does S/4HANA ensure the integrity of Actual Costing (Material Ledger) when the transformation hierarchy and component consumption occur within an external circular graph that does not report 1:1 consumptions in real time? How is multi‑level price determination handled when the “production receipt” in S/4HANA is decoupled from the actual physical transformation performed by the external system?
Based on this scenario, is it strictly necessary to rely on SAP IS‑Oil components (such as TSW or CPE) to maintain this physical‑economic reconciliation, or can this be achieved within standard S/4HANA?   

All recommendations are welcome.                                                                                                                                                                   

Best regards,

Gustavo H.

entry.title: Re: C_S4CFi- Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Clou...
entry.content:
Hi Zakaria,

I have done the certification today, so I can give you fresh observations 😊

So, after I confirmed that you want to start – and my 24h limit has started, I was given the pdf with tasks. At this point I still did not need to start the assessment itself!

So first I reviewed pdf – that contained 9 tasks altogether (first 2 were just to prepare fiori and add roles – same as in hands-ons) each task had some 2-4 subtasks, and I used some 2 hours to go through pdf and prepare some notes. When I was studying for exam, I went through complete learning journey and practiced all the hands-ons. The tasks in exam were similar, but I would say a little more complicated than hands-ons. For example, if in hands on I just created asset class, in certification I also needed to adjust useful life of the asset class (or at least I undestood I had to do that…)

After I was ready, I started the assessment itself (now 3-hours countdown started!) and enterred the systém. And here I really hesitated – while in hands-on I always new which Group number I am – that I need to use for ###, in certification this was nowhere stated! This was very unpleasant, and only as I new from hands-on, I automatically used last 3 digits from my user number, but I really felt no certainty about that!

The tasks themselves are reasonable, not too easy, but also not too difficult. You can use your notes, AI, you can search the web – all is allowed. What I did not like – I had some task where only in the end I realised that I made a mistake in master data, I had time to provide the task again, but I just could not correct myself, because the GL account with number dedicated to me was already created, posted on, and I could not correct it, nor delete it and start again.

Once done, I submitted – and web site wrote me message, that I have not started my assessment yet!!! Well – I just needed to refresh the page, and voila, then it worked, and I could stop officially – so it is probably wise not to leave this to the last minute, one can really panic on that…

I passed, but with lower percentage than I expected, based on my opinion of course 😊 – I had fulfilled all the tasks after all, but I have no detailed feedback – I do not know where I lost my points… But never mind, I am grateful that it is all behind me!

entry.title: SuccessFactors Onboarding translations not working dynamically for different locales
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have created an email template that is triggered to the hiring manager once the Review New Hire Data step is completed. The email is currently being sent only in English, even though Spanish and Japanese translations are already maintained in the system for this template.

The issue is that the translations are not being picked up based on the manager’s default language. Some managers use English as their default language, while others use Spanish, and we need the email to be delivered according to each manager’s selected locale. Although the translated content exists in the system, I am unable to configure the rule to support multiple languages. I tried using the language select rule, but I cannot retrieve the user’s default locale from the object. I’m unsure whether this capability is supported.

For context, we have two managers:

  • Manager 1 has English as their default language
  • Manager 2 has Spanish as their default language
Both managers are receiving the email, but when Manager 1 is assigned, the email should be sent to them in English, and when Manager 2 is assigned, the email should be sent to them in Spanish.

Could you please advise how this translation requirement can be achieved in the system? Any guidance or support would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks and regards,
Arvind S

entry.title: Re: Manufacturing in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Hello @Markus_Oertelt ; 

Thank you for this very content-rich release on Manufacturing, it is both useful and very interesting.

I particularly appreciated the “Combined Order” functionality, but I do have a few questions and comments :

 

1- Order Type Selection:
Why is it possible to manually choose an order type when creating the combined order? Could this lead to inconsistencies or conflicts with existing configuration settings ? Wouldn’t it be more consistent to automatically inherit the order type from the original orders being combined ?
 
2- Production Execution:
How is shop-floor execution managed in this context? Is it possible to confirm operations for both underlying orders, or is the confirmation process handled differently for the combined order ?
 
3- Target Activities at Operation Level:
Regarding machine time, labor time, and other activity types, how are target activity quantities determined from a PP and CO perspective ? Are the target values aggregated, and if so, what is the calculation logic behind the combined order ?
 
Looking forward to your clarification.
FENINA,Anas

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Onboarding Webcasts -Asia Pacific(Part 2)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the ‘Will you be attending?’ option will NOT register you for the event.

To register for this event, please click here: Register Here

SAP SuccessFactors: Part 2- Get Enabled and Prepare for Go Live (English, Asia Pacific)


As you move forward in your implementation journey with SuccessFactors, it's time to focus on enablement and preparing for a successful go-live. In this session, our experts will guide you through key enablement strategies, essential system validations, and final readiness steps to ensure a smooth deployment. Whether you're finalizing configurations or training your teams, this webinar will provide actionable insights to help you become empowered and deliver a successful go live.

Key Agenda Items:

  • Enablement Overview – A comprehensive guide to training resources by role
  • Best practices and Implementation guidance overview
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC) resources and readiness checks

entry.title: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA returns Success but MARC-MAABC and MARC-ABCIN do not update (S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am developing a report to mass update the ABC Indicator (MARC-MAABC) and the Cycle Counting/Physical Inventory Indicator (MARC-ABCIN) for existing materials across multiple plants (8001, 8021, etc.). 

The Issue: The function BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA runs without returning any Errors ('E') or Aborts ('A'). I see success messages, and I am calling BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT. However, when I check MM03 or the MARC table, the fields remain unchanged (empty). 

My code : 

FORM frm_update_master_data.
DATA: ls_headdata TYPE bapimathead,
            ls_plantdata TYPE bapi_marc,
            ls_plantdatax TYPE bapi_marcx,
            lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.

LOOP AT gt_output INTO DATA(ls_out).

        CLEAR: ls_headdata, ls_plantdata, ls_plantdatax, lt_return.

" --- Header ---
ls_headdata-material_long = ls_out-matnr.
ls_headdata-mrp_view = 'X'.
ls_headdata-storage_view = 'X'.

" --- Plant Data ---
ls_plantdata-plant = lv_plant.
ls_plantdatax-plant = lv_plant.

" ABC Indicator (MARC-MAABC)
ls_plantdata-abc_id = ls_out-abc_ind. " A, B, C
ls_plantdatax-abc_id = 'X'.

" XYZ/Cycle Counting (MARC-ABCIN)
 ls_plantdata-cc_ph_inv = ls_out-xyz_ind.  "A, B, D my system config
ls_plantdatax-cc_ph_inv = 'X'.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA'
EXPORTING
headdata = ls_headdata
plantdata = ls_plantdata
plantdatax = ls_plantdatax
TABLES
returnmessages = lt_return.

ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' EXPORTING wait = 'X'.
ENDLOOP.
ENDFORM.

Any advice is appreciated. Thanks!

entry.title: The Road Ahead: Predictions and Possibilities for the Future of Work
entry.content:
The future of work will not follow a single path—multiple, competing futures are already emerging.

This insightful session, led by Lauren Bidwell, Ph.D., a Senior Research Scientist at SAP SuccessFactors, offers a comprehensive exploration of this dynamic landscape. Dr. Bidwell will unpack ten key predictions structured around three core pillars: the Future of Working, which focuses on where and when work happens; the Future of the Workforce, examining who is doing the work; and the Future of Work Practices, which dives into how work is performed.

The presentation will also examine several diverging future scenarios, contrasting, for example, a future driven by pure AI-led productivity against one focused on AI-enabled human amplification. It will explore shifts from traditional career paths to models incorporating gig-based early careers or fostering home-grown talent development. Attendees will gain a clear understanding of these complex shifts. Crucially, the session will conclude by providing attendees with tangible next steps and actionable strategies, empowering them to actively craft a future of work that ensures prosperity for both human employees and their organizations.

Some Key Takeaways:
• Understand the diverse and sometimes conflicting paths ahead for the future of work and how these changes may impact your specific organization.

• Evaluate which of these emerging paths are most suitable for your organization's unique context and learn about the long-term, downstream implications of those choices.

• Implement concrete, tangible steps you can start taking immediately to begin crafting and achieving your desired future of work.

Register now

If you are not a member of HR.com, you will be prompted to create a free profile at www.hr.com. This will allow you to join a community of like-minded HR professionals, interact with them, and access educational content on the site. If you are having any issues, please contact info@hr.com

entry.title: The Hidden Threat to Your Clean Core: Variant Governance in S/4HANA
entry.content:
By Atul Joshi — Lead SAP Architect, Utilities & Clean Core Specialist

Introduction

Organizations invest millions in S/4HANA transformations, aiming for a Clean Core, agile upgrades, and a future‑proof digital landscape. We move custom code to BTP, rationalize interfaces, and modernize processes.

Yet one silent, often overlooked threat can undermine all of that work: uncontrolled variant changes in production batch jobs.

A single unauthorized variant change can cause incorrect billing, incomplete financial postings, or regulatory reporting issues. These failures are not technical—they are governance failures. And they can compromise Clean Core integrity even when no Z‑code exists.

Why Variant Governance Fails in Utilities and S/4HANA Landscapes

1. “Temporary Fix” Syndrome

A user changes a variant “just for today” and forgets to revert it.

2. Lack of Awareness

Users do not understand the downstream impact of altering a production variant.

3. Over‑Authorization

Security roles grant variant maintenance access where only execution is required.

The result is a silent failure that may not surface until weeks later—after financial cycles close, billing runs complete, or regulatory reports are submitted.

A Real‑World Case: The Variant That Broke a Month of Processing

A nightly batch report was scheduled using Variant X, configured to process all company codes. It ran successfully for months.

One day, a well‑meaning user modified Variant X and restricted it to a single company code, assuming it was a temporary adjustment.

No one noticed.

For the next month, the batch job processed only that one company code. No dumps, no warnings, no alerts. The issue surfaced only during reconciliation, when the business noticed missing postings across multiple entities.

By then:

  • Downstream reports were incorrect
  • Financial data was incomplete
  • Audit trails were inconsistent
  • Cleanup required days of effort

This was not a technical issue. It was a governance failure—and a perfect example of how a single variant change can undermine Clean Core stability.

Why This Threatens Clean Core

Clean Core is not only about removing custom code. It is about predictability, stability, and governance.

A single variant change can:

  • Break standardized processes
  • Trigger incorrect financial or billing results
  • Create audit and compliance risks
  • Force emergency patches
  • Introduce new technical debt

You can have zero Z‑code and still have a “dirty core” if your variants are uncontrolled.

My Approach to Variant Hardening

To address this risk, I use a three‑layer governance model that protects mission‑critical batch processes. This approach combines technical controls with operational discipline.

Layer 1: Protect Variant Flag — First Line of Defense

Every production variant should have the Protect Variant flag enabled (SE38 or program RSVARATT).

How it works:

Only the creator or last protector can modify the variant. Others can execute it, but fields become read‑only.

Benefit:

Prevents casual or accidental modifications.

Limitation:

It is a single point of failure—additional layers are required.

 

 

Layer 2: SAP Security Authorization — Granular Access Control

This is the enterprise‑grade safeguard.

Key object: S_PROGRAM

Key field: P_ACTION

Best practice:

  • Business users → SUBMIT only
  • Variant maintenance (VARIANT) → restricted to a small governance group

Impact:

Even if someone attempts to unprotect a variant, they cannot save changes without the correct authorization.

Layer 3: ABAP Validation — The Final Safety Net

For critical programs, embed validation logic inside the ABAP code.

Example:

abap

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.

  IF sy-batch = 'X'.

    IF s_company_code IS INITIAL.

      MESSAGE 'Company Code is mandatory for batch execution. Job aborted.' TYPE 'A'.

    ENDIF.

 

    IF p_date_from > p_date_to.

      MESSAGE 'Invalid date range. Job aborted.' TYPE 'A'.

    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

Result:

Even if a variant bypasses the first two layers, the program aborts before processing incorrect data.

The Executive Mandate: Variant Governance as a Clean Core Requirement

Variant governance must be elevated from a technical detail to a strategic governance priority.

As architects, we must:

  • Educate business teams
  • Enforce governance protocols
  • Treat variant control as part of Clean Core
  • Elevate this topic to leadership

The cost of prevention is minimal compared to the cost of correction.

By implementing this 3‑layer protocol, organizations can:

  • Protect data integrity
  • Ensure compliance
  • Improve operational stability
  • Safeguard their Clean Core investment

This is not just configuration—it is operational excellence.

Let’s Discuss

What challenges do you face in managing variants in your SAP landscape? Have you experienced a variant‑related incident?

Share your experiences below—let’s architect better governance together.

entry.title: [Webinar] Overview da Transição do SAP Solution Manager para o SAP Cloud ALM - Português
entry.content:
Neste webinar, apresentaremos uma visão geral da transição do SAP Solution Manager (SolMan) para o SAP Cloud ALM, abordando o contexto estratégico dessa mudança, o roadmap de descontinuação do SolMan e como as organizações podem se preparar de forma estruturada e segura.

 

Durante a sessão, serão discutidos os principais cenários de transição, incluindo coexistência entre SolMan e SAP Cloud ALM, critérios para migração de processos, além das diferenças funcionais entre as duas soluções no gerenciamento do ciclo de vida das aplicações SAP.

 

Os participantes também terão uma introdução ao SAP Cloud ALM, entendendo seu posicionamento como plataforma central de ALM para ambientes SAP cloud, híbridos e on-premise, cobrindo capacidades de Implementação, Operações, Monitoramento e Integrações, bem como boas práticas para iniciar a adoção.

 

O webinar é indicado para profissionais de TI, consultores SAP, arquitetos de solução e equipes de ALM, que desejam compreender o impacto da transição do SolMan, definir uma estratégia de evolução e planejar os próximos passos.

 

Pré-requisitos

Solicitamos que você complete as seguintes etapas antes de participar do evento:

  1. Realize o processo de ativação do seu tenant. Acesse o link para ativar seu SAP Cloud ALM (caso ainda não tenha sido provisionado): https://me.sap.com/systemsprovisioning/provisioning.
  2. Como Solicitar SAP Cloud ALM no SAP for Me - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gxfyDFGYT2g
  3. Lembre-se do ID de usuário e da senha que foram usados para reivindicar o tenant do SAP Cloud ALM e também para o cockpit do BTP.
  4. Link para acessar o cockpit do BTP - https://account.hana.ondemand.com/#/home/welcome
  5. Verifique o e-mail de notification@sap.com para o link de acesso ao seu SAP Cloud ALM e os próximos passos.
  6. Link para acessar Configuração e Administração para SAP Cloud ALM - Configuração da Fundação  https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/enabling-api.

entry.title: [Webinar] Overview de la Transición de SAP Solution Manager a SAP Cloud ALM – Español
entry.content:
En este webinar, presentaremos una visión general de la transición de SAP Solution Manager (SolMan) a SAP Cloud ALM, abordando el contexto estratégico de este cambio, el roadmap de descontinuación de SolMan y cómo las organizaciones pueden prepararse de manera estructurada y segura.

 

Durante la sesión, se analizarán los principales escenarios de transición, incluyendo la coexistencia entre SolMan y SAP Cloud ALM, los criterios para la migración de procesos, así como las diferencias funcionales entre ambas soluciones en la gestión del ciclo de vida de las aplicaciones SAP.

 

Los participantes también recibirán una introducción a SAP Cloud ALM, comprendiendo su posicionamiento como la plataforma central de ALM para entornos SAP cloud, híbridos y on-premise, cubriendo capacidades de Implementación, Operaciones, Monitoreo e Integraciones, además de buenas prácticas para iniciar su adopción.

 

Requisitos previos:

Le solicitamos que complete los siguientes pasos antes de participar en el evento:

  1. Realice el proceso de activación de su tenant. Acceda al enlace para activar su SAP Cloud ALM (en caso de que aún no haya sido aprovisionado): https://me.sap.com/systemsprovisioning/provisioning.
  2. Cómo Solicitar SAP Cloud ALM en SAP for Me: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gxfyDFGYT2g.
  3. Recuerde el ID de usuario y la contraseña que se utilizaron para reclamar el tenant de SAP Cloud ALM y también para el cockpit del BTP.
  4. Enlace para acceder al cockpit del BTP: https://account.hana.ondemand.com/#/home/welcome.
  5. Verifique el correo electrónico de notification@sap.com para el enlace de acceso a su SAP Cloud ALM y próximos pasos.

Enlace para acceder a Configuración y Administración para SAP Cloud ALM - Configuración de la Fundación: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/enabling-api.

entry.title: Re: Deletion of CI_COBL
entry.content:
Hi,

An error was reported. Details are as follows:

When transaction code FBL1H was executed, the program terminated with a short dump.
Error details:
"invalidated view: V_GLPOS_C_VEN_CT: line 1 col 155 (at pos 154) [dbdbslst 5958] Exception = CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB_Sql_Error, program = FAGL_LIB_PIVB=================CM00W, line = 88"

After analyzing the error, I found that it was caused by the ZZ* field in the CI_GLPOSCCT customer include, which is used in the V_GLPOS* views.

Solution:
I deleted the CI_GLPOSCCT include via SE11 and then regenerated the views using transaction code HDBVIEWS.

entry.title: Indeed’s Transition Away from XML Feeds: What SAP SuccessFactors Customers Need to Know
entry.content:
In July 2025, Indeed announced a strategic shift away from XML-based job feeds for all new customer career site integrations and third-party delivery methods. This direction was further formalized in October 2025, when Indeed notified customers of its plan to discontinue “single-source feeds” entirely effective March 31, 2026 for organic (free) job postings, and by the end of 2026 for sponsored delivery.

This change applies broadly across all recruiting platforms, including SAP SuccessFactors, and requires customers to transition to either a multi-source feed model or a direct ATS integration.

Indeed’s Strategic Direction

This transition is being driven and governed by Indeed as part of its move toward direct delivery integrations and expanded adoption of the Indeed Apply workflow. While Indeed supports a multi-source feed approach in general, SAP SuccessFactors does not currently offer an organic multi-source feed option. The connection with Recruiting Posting and Indeed is a multi-source feed, however it is intended for sponsored jobs only and would not solve for the immediate deadline. Instead, customers can leverage a direct API-based integration with Indeed, which has been established in collaboration between Indeed’s product team and SAP’s services organization.

Because this is an Indeed-directed integration, all process documentation and enablement are managed directly by Indeed. Indeed will work with customers to align requisition and application templates so the apply experience mirrors the Indeed workflow. The objective is to provide candidates with a seamless, single-platform application experience within Indeed, while enabling candidate data to be imported directly into SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting (RCM) after apply.

Reporting and Analytics Considerations

One notable impact of this approach affects customers using Career Site Builder. Since candidates will apply directly through Indeed and bypass the career site, Indeed will no longer appear as a source in Career Site Builder Advanced Analytics reporting. Customers should consider this reporting change as part of their broader transition planning as they will only be able to view the Indeed source in the candidate reporting within Recruiting Management.

Key Deadlines and Customer Actions

According to Indeed’s communications, customers who have not transitioned to the direct API integration by March 31, 2026 will no longer have their organic job feeds collected, and their free job postings will be removed from Indeed. At this time, it is unclear whether a grace period will be offered beyond this date.
Customers who wish to maintain organic job visibility on Indeed are strongly encouraged to contact their Indeed account representative as soon as possible to begin the transition. Early engagement is important to ensure placement in Indeed’s integration queue for the new API connection.

Support Model and Implementation Timeline

Integration documentation is available to employers directly through the Indeed portal, and links can be shared to guide customers through the required steps. SAP Support will not be directly involved in the transition, as the API connection must be established and validated with Indeed to meet their integration requirements.
If additional assistance is needed, customers may engage their SAP Account Executive or Customer Success Manager to explore a light services engagement, or coordinate with their implementation partner or consulting team. On average, the end-to-end integration timeline is approximately four to six weeks, with kickoff timing dependent on the availability of Indeed’s integration team and customer resources.

Indeed Integrations for Employers 

ATS Sync for SuccessFactors Installation Guide

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - APIM - Domain-Centric Routing Pattern (DCRP): Governing APIs via CPI Provider - Part I
entry.content:
@morteninge 

Thank you for your question. I want to clarify an important point. What I am proposing is not a product feature found in a manual. It is an Architectural Design Pattern called Domain-Centric Routing (DCRP).

While SAP Standard documentation provides the basic components and dynamic routing mechanism, like how to use KVMs or JavaScript policies, it doesn’t always outline the overall approach for large-scale governance.

To address your question:
1. Is it a Best Practice? Yes, from an Enterprise Architecture viewpoint. Exposing CPI through APIM is a standard recommendation from SAP to ensure security (OAuth/SAML), throttling, and analytics.
2. Is this specific routing pattern in the official docs? No. The official docs explain how dynamic routing works, but the DCRP framework is an innovation designed to address 'Proxy Sprawl.' This is a common issue in large landscapes that the standard one-to-one approach does not solve.

In short, we are using SAP standard tools to create a non-standard, high-efficiency architecture.

Kind regards,

Viana | The commander.

Enterprise Integration Archtect | SAP BTP Integration Suite

entry.title: Re: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 1)
entry.content:
Hi Sebastiano,

I asked this on BlueSky and Mauricio gave his opinion, but I think it's worth a question to the Champions.

So this is a question about how many CIS tenants for a medium to large Dev, QAS and Production landscape...

SuccessFactors has a best practice that says, because SF is the import source for employees (mainly to allow you to use embedded SAC apparently) - that you really need a CIS tier per SF instance (and you get an additional one for free with SuccessFactors for this reason).

But the general recommendation from SAP is 2-tiers and sharing the non-production between Dev/QAS but I wonder if they would take that stance if you think about S/4HANA Public Cloud where Developers should apparently go nowhere near production data in QAS (extreme statement that I hear often). 

I totally get that the admin is lower for 2-tiers with certificates, access requests, etc; but then it does mean going down the path of _dev and _qas roles which would not match up with production roles.

Also, it is easy to accidentally give someone access to QAS data via this path (especially if you've gone the whole hog and using it to provision to the back-end S/4HANA system).

I would think it would be better from a CIS and BTP tenant perspective to create AD Groups like MyAppAdmin, MyAppAdmin_CISDEV, MyAppAdmin_CISQAS - and within 3 tenants, filter on *_CISDEV or *_CISQAS on Dev and QAS CIS tenants respectively, and then map identically in Dev, QAS and PROD to the same name in BTP (obviously different CIS tenant being the only difference).

This way there is 0 chance of accidentally assigning QAS access to someone who should not have this access.

Mauricio's response was:
I think 2-tier is easier to support (1 dev/qas tenant + 1 prd), but also easier to make mistakes in the dev/qas tenant if everyone touches it...

I think as soon as you say, it is easier to make mistakes in the dev/qas tenant - then the fact that qas likely points at production copied data - starts to make me think that as painful as the extra maintenance is, it might be necessary if you really want to be focused on cyber security.

Also, it would be nice to let developers create test users in Development and assign roles directly but a 2-tier landscape does not really support that scenario safely.

So my question - Is the position of a 2-tier CIS tenant still practically best practice if you care about cyber security (which we all need to nowadays)?

Cheers,

Matt

entry.title: BiWhy – Files
entry.content:
Disclaimer:
BiWhy is available again for FREE to everyone on biwhy.net

Problem

  • One node in the system has issues while others work fine
  • Need to compare installations between servers
  • Need to analyze file system state
  • Repeated folder searches are required – OS search is slow if folders are not indexed

Inconsistent nodes are often caused by small file differences:

  • Private patch left on one node
  • Different library version
  • Missing or modified essential file

This situation is more common than expected.

Solution

BiWhy quickly indexes selected folders and allows comparison:

  • Between different servers
  • Between different points in time

Nonessential files (logs, tmp, dumps, etc.) can be filtered out (positive or negative filters).

Server-specific folder or file names can be handled without breaking comparison logic.

Results

Comparison output is available as:

  • Tree view
  • Table view

With:

  • File count
  • Size
  • Modification details
  • Highlighting or hiding differences
  • Embedded BiWhy analytics

This enables fast identification of structural or version inconsistencies.

Configuration Differences

Issues between nodes are often caused by configuration files.

For that BiWhy has dedicated “mass configuration comparison” functionality that compares configuration across multiple files.

Details: https://biwhy.net/post/compare-configuration

Screenshots

Raw comparison, ~650k files of ~70GB:

After hiding common files and filtering some common log extension, ~50k files and ~10GB. Further go to subfolders, filter more temporary or log files, filter even more in and compare remaining few files in table by size, modification or just missing files.

entry.title: Unlock S/4HANA Cloud Secrets: Hybrid Events+APIs Strategy for Seamless BTP Data Flows
entry.content:
Strategic Financial Data Extraction from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to SAP BTP

Using Transfer Pricing as a Real-World Example

Transfer pricing represents a perfect use case to demonstrate generic financial data extraction strategies from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Multinational organisations need GL, Controlling, Material Ledger, and Asset Accounting postings to flow reliably to external engines hosted on SAP BTP—but Public Cloud’s “no direct database access” constraint demands architecturally sound patterns.

Why Transfer Pricing Perfectly Illustrates the Challenge

The business need: Calculate inter-company markups (cost-plus, resale-minus) using real-time ACDOCA postings across multiple currencies, profit centres, and cost elements.
The constraint: Pure cloud extensibility—no RFCs, no custom ABAP, no direct table extracts. 
The solution: Three proven patterns that work for any financial data integration scenario.

Pattern 1: Event Notifications (Real-Time Push Model)

How transfer pricing uses it: GL postings trigger automatic business event notifications routed through SAP Event Mesh to BTP applications.

 

Event Notification

Generic applicability: Works for any posting-driven process—revenue recognition, inter-company reconciliation, compliance monitoring. 

High-level activation:
  1. Integration configuration apps → Activate accounting-related event notifications
  2. Communication scenarios → Configure outbound event destinations
  3. Event Mesh subscription → Point to BTP service endpoint
  4. Payload contains: Company codes, ledger amounts (local/group), profit centres, material valuations

Pattern 2: Standard Query Services (Scheduled Pull Model)

How transfer pricing uses it: BTP service queries S/4HANA nightly for new/changed financial postings.

 

Query ServiceKey services (release-dependent availability):

  • Financial line item services → GL/CO/ML/AA postings
  • Profitability services → Margin analysis data
  • Material valuation services → Cost component details

Generic applicability: Perfect for scheduled reconciliation, historical backfills, or validation runs.

Pattern 3: Hybrid Extraction (Production Resilience)

Transfer pricing demands 99.9% coverage. Smart architects layer all three patterns:

Use CasePrimary PatternTransfer Pricing ExampleGeneric Use
Real-time GL postingsEvent notificationsInvoice → immediate markupAny posting trigger
Historical catch-upStandard APIsMonth-end reconciliationData migration
Day 1 implementationBulk extractionFull ACDOCA baselineInitial loads
Custom calculationsBTP processingException overridesBusiness rules

 Three-tier reference flow:

Hybrid ExtractionBTP Implementation Patterns (Transfer Pricing Example)

Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
service FinancialIntegration {
  entity Postings as projection on external financial services;
  entity PricingResults as projection on HANA staging;
}

 Pattern: Event handlers + scheduled queries → unified HANA staging → business rules.

ABAP RESTful Application Programming (RAP)
define root view entity FinancialDataView
  as select from standard financial services

Pattern: Analytical CDS views over external data → transactional services.

Architect’s Production Checklist

✅ Tenant validation: Test events/APIs in your specific S/4HANA Cloud environment
✅ Quota management: Monitor Event Mesh throughput + API rate limits
✅ Fallback design: API polling validates event stream completeness
✅ Quarterly readiness: SAP evolves event coverage and API fields continuously
✅ Pure cloud: Zero custom code in production S/4HANA

The Universal Pattern

Transfer pricing proves the strategy works for complex financial scenarios. The same architecture applies to:
  • Inter-company reconciliation
  • Revenue recognition automation
  • Compliance reporting engines
  • Analytics data pipelines
  • Any standard data object/model driven process

Universal PatternKey takeaway: This isn’t “just for transfer pricing”—it’s your reusable blueprint for any S/4HANA Cloud → BTP data integration.

entry.title: Re: Request to activate S-User
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If the new S-user ID did not receive the activation e-mail, check the user's e-mail address in the User Management application under the Requested Users tab. Then the user can use the Forgot Password link detailed in KBA 1808560 to set the password.

If the user still does not receive the e-mail, please, create a case with XX-SER-FORME.

More detailed information is documented in KBA 2490845 - New S-user ID did not receive a Welcome e-mail to set their password - SAP for Me.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Manage Sales Documents App: Streamline Your Sales Management
entry.content:
We are excited to introduce the new Manage Sales Documents app, designed to transform how internal sales representatives handle sales orders and orders without charge. With this app, you as an internal sales representative can manage daily sales tasks with greater speed and ease.

Key Learning Outcomes

You will be able to:

  • Understand the basic features of the app.
  • Use the app to filter sales document headers, items, and schedule lines.
  • Use the navigation options and actions in the list report.

Use Case

As an internal sales rep, you need to manage sales orders and sales orders without charge more efficiently. With the Manage Sales Documents app, tasks that were previously time-consuming are now automated and simplified. Intuitive filtering options allow you to apply criteria across sales document headers, items, or schedule lines. The filtered results are displayed in the worklist for further operations. Additionally, you can perform quick actions on selected entries, and navigate to related applications.

IAM Information

  • Business Catalog: SAP_SD_BC_SDL_PRC_PC
  • Business Role Template: SAP_BR_INTERNAL_SALES_REP, SAP_BR_SALES_MANAGER

App Overview

The Manage Sales Documents app features a list report.

The table area displays three tabs:

  • Sales Documents
  • Sales Document Items
  • Schedule Lines

Default filters

These filters can take default values from user settings.

Frequently used fields are shown by default. Header fields are also visible in the items tab, and item and header fields in the schedule lines tab.

  • Default fields on Sales Documents tab

  • Default fields on Sales Document Items tab

 

  • Default fields on Schedule Lines tab

  • Optimized product display - separate Product and Product Description

     

More fields are available in the View Settings that can be adapted according to individual needs.

Actions

The app provides a rich set of action buttons:

  • Create Sales Documents

    • You can create sales orders and sales orders without charge.
    • For sales order types like CCFU, CCIS, CCLN, and OR, you navigate to the Manage Sales Orders- Version 2 app for the creation.
    • For the other sales order types, you navigate to the Create Sales Orders – VA01 app for the creation.

       

    • For sales order without charge type CBFD, you navigate to the Manage Sales Orders Without Charge - Version 2 app for the creation.
    • For the other sales order without charge types, you navigate to the Create Sales Order Without Charge – VA01 app for the creation.

  • Actions on Sales Documents Tab

    • Delivery Block: Set and remove delivery blocks.
    • Billing Block: Set and remove billing blocks.
    • Additional options include Reject All Items, Update Prices, and Check Credit.

  • Actions on Sales Document Items Tab

    • Includes Delivery Block, Billing Block and Rejection Reason settings as well as Update Prices.

       

       

  • Actions on Schedule Lines Tab

    • Options to set or remove delivery blocks.

The actions are processed as synchronized batch operations, with updates applied to the relevant fields. Messages indicating operation success or failure are displayed.

Smart Links

Smart Links provide seamless navigation to related applications:

  • From a sales document number, you can open related apps.
  • From the sold-to party, ship-to party, bill-to party, or payer, you can open related apps.
  • Overall Status on Sales Documents tab supports process flow insights.
  • The Product field allows quick access to related apps.

Navigation to Document Details

The app supports navigation to object pages based on the document type.

  • For sales order types such as CCFU, CCIS, CCLN, and OR, you can navigate to the document object page in the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app. 
  • For the order without charge type CBFD,  you can navigate to the document object page in the Manage Sales Orders Without Charge - Version 2 app. 
  • From an entry in the item or schedule line tab, you can navigate to the object page of the selected document item.
  • For sales document types other than CCFU, CCIS, CCLN, OR, and CBFD, you can navigate to the Display Sales Orders - VA03 or Display Sales Orders Without Charge - VA03 app.

Data Export

For offline analysis, users can export sales document data (in the displayed format) to Excel.

entry.title: 更透明、更可控：SAP S/4HANA 数据迁移 Direct Transfer 引入“暂存表”是什么？为什么重要？
entry.content:
在企业上S/4HANA Cloud 的过程中，“数据迁移”几乎是所有项目里最容易出问题、但又最关键的一环。
如果数据迁得不好，业务上线就可能卡壳，甚至推迟 go-live。

过去，SAP 提供了一种叫 Direct Transfer（从 SAP 源系统直接迁移） 的方式，它可以让系统直接从 ECC 等 SAP 系统取数据到 S/4HANA Cloud，省去了模板文件、人工准备的麻烦。

听起来很方便，对吧？但 Direct Transfer 一直有两个“痛点”：

  • 数据怎么选的？看不清！
  • 想改一点数据？不好改！

SAP 在最新2602版本中推出了一个新功能：
“暂存表作为中间存储（Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage）”
它专门用来解决这些痛点。

下面我们用最简单的方式解释这个功能。

1. 为什么要引入“暂存表”？

可以把 Direct Transfer 想象成“快递直发”模式：
源系统往 S/4HANA Cloud直接投递数据，不停留、不中转。

但业务团队的问题是：

  • 我怎么知道快递里到底有哪些东西？
  • 要是想在上车之前检查一下、重新整理一下怎么办？

所以 SAP 新增了“中转仓（暂存表）”：

数据先暂存一下 → 你可以打开看看 → 想改还能改 → 再继续往 S/4HANA Cloud 送。

这样做带来了两个很大的改变：

✔ 透明度提升

你终于可以完整看到系统“选中的数据”。

✔ 可控性提升

你可以在暂存表里直接调整数据，例如修正业务字段、清洗数据等。
不再是“系统决定你只能接受哪些数据”。

这对业务团队来说非常实用，因为上线前总会有字段需要微调。

2. 它是怎么工作的？

过去：
源系统 → 直接 → S/4HANA Cloud（如下图）

现在：
源系统 → 暂存表（你可查看/修改） → S/4HANA Cloud（如下图）

整个过程被拆成两步： 

步骤 1：Extract to Staging Tables（抽取到暂存表）

源系统的选定数据先被放进远程 HANA schema 的暂存表里。

步骤 2：Transfer from Staging Tables（从暂存表传入 S/4HANA Cloud 迁移应用）

你确认数据没问题后，再继续送入 Migrate Your Data 应用。

重点：你可以在步骤 1 → 步骤 2 之间“开箱检查”。

3. 业务人员能从中获得什么好处？

以下是业务视角的真实收益：

✔ 1. 更容易“审查数据”

以前选数结果看不见，只能依赖 IT 或顾问。
现在可以 打开表直接看，业务参与度大大提高。 

✔ 2. 出错更早暴露

因为能在暂存表直接看到数据，你可以：

  • 立刻发现不该被选中的记录
  • 立刻发现字段错填、主数据缺失等问题

避免到了模拟或正式迁移时才掉坑。

✔ 3. 灵活调整，减少返工

例如你发现：

  • 某条数据的标识字段应该更新
  • 描述字段需要统一格式
  • 某些数据需要从迁移范围中剔除

以前要写规则、找技术人员调整。
现在在暂存表里直接改，很快就能解决。

✔ 4. 有利于跨部门协作

暂存表可以被业务、数据治理团队、IT、顾问同时参考，
成为迁移前的“公共审查区”，减少沟通成本。

4. 什么时候适合启用这个功能？

如果你的迁移项目中具有以下特征之一，建议优先启用：

  • 迁移对象数据量大、字段复杂
  • 业务对数据质量要求高
  • 跨部门数据确认流程繁琐
  • 需要提前抽样审核数据
  • 过去 Direct Transfer 老是出错，但原因不明

5. 常见疑问（FAQ）

Q1：启用暂存表后，我是否必须每次都去编辑数据库？

不是。
暂存表是“可选的、按需使用”的。
你可以：

  • 什么都不改（只用来查看）
  • 或者只改关键字段
  • 或仅做抽样审核

完全取决于你的流程和数据质量。

Q2：这种暂存表和“使用暂存表迁移”的暂存表是同一个吗？

不是！
两种暂存表有完全不同的用途和结构，不能混用。 

Q3：会不会很难启用？

一点也不难。
在创建迁移项目时的 Step 3：Staging Tables（Optional） 打开即可。


并按需为迁移对象启用。

6. 小结：为什么这个新功能值得关注？

一句话总结：

它让 SAP 数据迁移第一次实现了可视、可控、可调整。

对于业务团队来说，这意味着：

  • 更少的不确定性
  • 更少的返工
  • 更清晰的上线路径

对于项目整体来说，则是质量提升 + 风险下降的双赢。

这个特性特别适合数据复杂、对质量敏感、跨团队协作密集的企业。

 

参考链接：SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Direc... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Release Standard Price in Valuation Typ 01 (Own Production)
entry.content: I am also facing the same issue. After enabling batch splitting, I found that the preliminary cost for some materials was incorrect.
